description: A flat dish from which food is eaten or served; a surface for presenting nourishment. 'Palu' names what presents—the flat offering of prepared food.
sound_symbolism: The word presents flat. 'Pa' opens with gentle offering, surface ready. Then 'lu' flows outward, plate extending welcome.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo palu' (the plate). Flat unlike 'noru' (bowl)—for solid foods, for sharing.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Plates present food—the arranged meal, the shared platter, hospitality made visible.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Sharing from one plate—the communal dish that builds connection through food.
